designSystem.styleGuidelines:
## Brand & Style
The design system is engineered to project the authority of mobile engineering rather than a traditional neighborhood garage. The brand personality is clinical, rugged, and uncompromisingly professional. It targets high-end vehicle owners and commercial fleets who value technical expertise and efficiency.

The visual style is **Industrial Minimalism** mixed with **Blueprint Aesthetics**. It utilizes a structured, technical layout inspired by automotive schematics. Key characteristics include:
- **Asymmetrical Balance:** Breaking standard centered layouts to create a sense of forward motion and mechanical complexity.
- **Technical Precision:** Use of fine lines (0.5pt to 1pt) to mimic technical drawings and engineering plans.
- **High Contrast:** A "Dark Mode First" approach that emphasizes the "St. Louis Asphalt" and "High-Vis Amber" relationship to ensure critical information is impossible to miss.

## Layout & Spacing
The layout follows a **Rigid Blueprint Grid**.
- **Grid Model:** A 12-column fixed grid for desktop, but with intentionally "broken" alignment. Elements may start on Column 2 and end on Column 11 to create whitespace "gutters" that feel like technical margins.
- **Section Dividers:** Instead of background color changes, use thin `Tool Steel` lines with `label-mono` annotations (e.g., "SEC_01 // DIAGNOSTICS") in the top left corner of the border.
- **Vertical Rhythm:** Large vertical gaps (80px–120px) between sections to create a premium, editorial feel.

## Elevation & Depth
Depth is created through **Layered Outlines** rather than soft shadows.
- **Flat Depth:** Avoid blurred shadows. Use "1px Solid" borders for cards.
- **The "Blueprint" Stack:** Overlap images and text boxes with a 2px offset. For example, a card might have a secondary "ghost" border behind it offset by 4px to the bottom-right.
- **Glassmorphism:** Use sparingly for overlays (like "Engine Status" popups). Apply a heavy background blur (20px) with a subtle `Tool Steel` tint at 10% opacity.

## Components
- **Primary Buttons:** High-Vis Amber background with Black text. Sharp corners. On hover, the button should "fill" with a Tool Steel border or shift 2px for a tactile "click" effect.
- **Input Fields:** Thick bottom border (2px) in Tool Steel. Labels use the `label-mono` style floating strictly above the field.
- **Cards:** No background (transparent) with a 1px Tool Steel border. Header areas of cards should have a subtle texture or a "crosshair" icon in the corners.
- **Status Chips:** Small, rectangular, with monochromatic backgrounds (High-Vis Amber for active, Tool Steel for pending).
- **The "Diagnostic" List:** List items separated by dotted lines (blueprint style) with monospaced data on the right.
- **Specialty Component - "The Schematic View":** An image container that features thin leader lines pointing to specific parts of a vehicle, using `label-mono` for callouts.

---

## Brand & Style
The design system is engineered to project the authority of mobile engineering rather than a traditional neighborhood garage. The brand personality is clinical, rugged, and uncompromisingly professional. It targets high-end vehicle owners and commercial fleets who value technical expertise and efficiency.

The visual style is **Industrial Minimalism** mixed with **Blueprint Aesthetics**. It utilizes a structured, technical layout inspired by automotive schematics. Key characteristics include:
- **Asymmetrical Balance:** Breaking standard centered layouts to create a sense of forward motion and mechanical complexity.
- **Technical Precision:** Use of fine lines (0.5pt to 1pt) to mimic technical drawings and engineering plans.
- **High Contrast:** A "Dark Mode First" approach that emphasizes the "St. Louis Asphalt" and "High-Vis Amber" relationship to ensure critical information is impossible to miss.

## Colors
The palette is rooted in the materials of the trade.
- **St. Louis Asphalt (#1A1C1E):** The primary foundation. Used for backgrounds and heavy structural elements to evoke the road and durability.
- **High-Vis Amber (#FBBF24):** The action color. Reserved for primary CTAs, critical status indicators, and highlights. It demands attention against the dark base.
- **Tool Steel (#475569):** Used for secondary information, icons, and schematic lines. It provides a cool, metallic contrast.
- **Pure White (#FFFFFF / #F8FAFC):** Used exclusively for high-readability body text and essential labels.

## Typography
Typography reflects mechanical marking and editorial hierarchy.
- **Headlines:** Oswald is utilized for its condensed, vertical stature, reminiscent of vintage automotive badges and industrial signage. Always uppercase for primary headings.
- **Body:** Inter provides a neutral, high-legibility counterpart to the aggressive headlines, ensuring technical descriptions are easy to digest.
- **Data/Labels:** JetBrains Mono (monospaced) is used for technical specs, serial numbers, and "blueprint" annotations to reinforce the engineering vibe.

## Layout & Spacing
The layout follows a **Rigid Blueprint Grid**.
- **Grid Model:** A 12-column fixed grid for desktop, but with intentionally "broken" alignment. Elements may start on Column 2 and end on Column 11 to create whitespace "gutters" that feel like technical margins.
- **Section Dividers:** Instead of background color changes, use thin `Tool Steel` lines with `label-mono` annotations (e.g., "SEC_01 // DIAGNOSTICS") in the top left corner of the border.
- **Vertical Rhythm:** Large vertical gaps (80px–120px) between sections to create a premium, editorial feel.

## Elevation & Depth
Depth is created through **Layered Outlines** rather than soft shadows.
- **Flat Depth:** Avoid blurred shadows. Use "1px Solid" borders for cards.
- **The "Blueprint" Stack:** Overlap images and text boxes with a 2px offset. For example, a card might have a secondary "ghost" border behind it offset by 4px to the bottom-right.
- **Glassmorphism:** Use sparingly for overlays (like "Engine Status" popups). Apply a heavy background blur (20px) with a subtle `Tool Steel` tint at 10% opacity.

## Shapes
The design system uses a **Strict Zero-Radius** policy. 
- **Sharp Corners:** Every button, input field, and card must have 90-degree sharp corners to evoke the precision of machined steel.
- **Diagonal Shears:** Use 45-degree clipped corners for primary buttons or status chips to mimic industrial warning plates.

## Components
- **Primary Buttons:** High-Vis Amber background with Black text. Sharp corners. On hover, the button should "fill" with a Tool Steel border or shift 2px for a tactile "click" effect.
- **Input Fields:** Thick bottom border (2px) in Tool Steel. Labels use the `label-mono` style floating strictly above the field.
- **Cards:** No background (transparent) with a 1px Tool Steel border. Header areas of cards should have a subtle texture or a "crosshair" icon in the corners.
- **Status Chips:** Small, rectangular, with monochromatic backgrounds (High-Vis Amber for active, Tool Steel for pending).
- **The "Diagnostic" List:** List items separated by dotted lines (blueprint style) with monospaced data on the right.
- **Specialty Component - "The Schematic View":** An image container that features thin leader lines pointing to specific parts of a vehicle, using `label-mono` for callouts.

screens.name: projects/5470218556025389168/screens/a656cb8dadc14351af4be94c65e1f12b
screens.prompt:
Build a complete, high-end editorial desktop landing page for BH MOBILE MECHANIC LLC. Design aesthetic: "Industrial Precision". Use the established dark, high-contrast palette (#101415 background with high-vis amber #ffb900 accents). Layout & Sections: 1. Header: Use the TopNavBar component with phone number (314-224-2945). 2. Hero: High-impact section featuring {{DATA:IMAGE:IMAGE_3}}. Title: "MECHANICAL SUPERIORITY. DELIVERED ONSITE." CTA: "REQUEST DISPATCH". 3. Credibility: A technical grid showing key metrics: "EST. 2024 STL", "045 MIN AVG LATENCY", "100% MOBILE". 4. Services/Offerings: Use a blueprint-style grid for "DIAGNOSTICS", "ROADSIDE REPAIR", "FLEET MAINTENANCE", and "ENGINE OPTIMIZATION". Include {{DATA:IMAGE:IMAGE_6}} as a precision detail asset. 5. About/Manifesto: A grit-and-engineering section titled "ST. LOUIS INDUSTRIAL ENGINEERING DIVISION" with {{DATA:IMAGE:IMAGE_2}}. Content should emphasize precision over a traditional garage model. 6. Visit/Logistics: Titled "COMMAND CENTER" at 4057 Meramec Street, STL. MUST include the specific map slot:
. No placeholder images for the map. 7. Contact: A technical "SERVICE LOG ENTRY" form for dispatch requests including operator name, vehicle identification, and diagnostic summary. 8. Footer: Complete company footer with copyright and technical links. Technical requirements: Use the Oswald font from the design system, semantic HTML, and custom CSS properties for the "St. Louis Asphalt" and "High-Vis Amber" theme. No lorem ipsum. Use the contact details: (314) 224-2945, Afrounitedtz@gmail.com, 4057 Meramec Street, St. Louis, MO 63116. ## Available Images You MUST include the following images in this design. You must reference them by putting their exact placeholder IDs in `src` attributes or CSS `background-image` rules. Do NOT use external URLs or generate new placeholders. Preserve these IDs exactly as written. Read each image's Description to understand its subject, intended section, and visual role. - {{DATA:IMAGE:IMAGE_2}} Description: A gritty, industrial portrait of a St. Louis street corner with brick warehouses in the background, desaturated colors, high contrast. A slight motion blur suggesting speed and reliability. Moody, cinematic atmosphere. 8k resolution. Dimensions: 1200x896 Aspect Ratio: 1.34 - {{DATA:IMAGE:IMAGE_3}} Description: A high-end, editorial hero image for a mobile mechanic brand. A clean, matte-black mobile service van parked in a gritty but cinematic St. Louis industrial area at dusk. The lighting is dramatic, highlighting the 'BH Mobile Mechanic' branding on the van. The style is professional photography, moody, with high contrast and a cool, desaturated color grade. High-vis amber light reflects off the wet asphalt. No people. 8k resolution, cinematic composition. Dimensions: 1376x768 Aspect Ratio: 1.79 - {{DATA:IMAGE:IMAGE_6}} Description: Close-up, high-detail macro shot of professional mechanical tools (wrenches, diagnostic tablet) laid out with clinical precision on a clean, dark industrial workbench. The lighting is crisp, cold, and professional. Deep shadows, high-vis amber accents in the tool grips. Industrial precision aesthetic. 8k resolution. Dimensions: 1264x848 Aspect Ratio: 1.49

screens.title: BH Mobile Mechanic - Industrial Precision Landing Page

outputComponents.text:
I have completed the production-ready desktop landing page for **BH Mobile Mechanic LLC**, fully implementing the **Industrial Precision** aesthetic.

### Key Features:
- **Editorial Visuals**: Integrated the high-fidelity cinematic assets (the mobile unit in STL, precision tools, and the industrial warehouse) to establish a premium, engineering-focused brand identity.
- **Technical Grid Layout**: Designed an asymmetrical, blueprint-inspired layout that uses oversized condensed typography and high-contrast "St. Louis Asphalt" and "High-Vis Amber" tones.
- **Service Protocols**: Structured the services section as a set of technical protocols rather than a generic menu, emphasizing mechanical superiority and diagnostic precision.
- **Command Center**: Included the requested logistics section with the exact `data-map-slot` requirements and a professional "Service Log Entry" dispatch form.
- **Performance & Semantics**: Built with clean, semantic HTML and CSS custom properties, ensuring the site is scalable and maintains its high-end editorial feel across devices.

The design is now live on your canvas for review. How would you like to proceed?

outputComponents.suggestion: Add subtle scroll animations to the technical grid
Create a mobile-optimized version of this page
Add a 'Fleet Protocols' detailed service page
